slide_1_Cover: Audit-Led Mechanical Royalty Recovery for Songwriters
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: handa identifies exact uncollected dollar amounts before asking for a commitment; zero-upfront, 20% success-based model removes the entry barriers created by incumbents like Songtrust ($100 signup + 15–20% fee), perfectly aligning handa’s incentives with independent creators
Mechanicals-first strategy targets $500-800 per 1M streams versus $100 for performance — founder with 100M+ streams built product from publishing ops experience
30-second catalog audit uses MLC 54M-work database and AI matching to show songwriters the exact dollar amount they are losing — 34% conversion at zero cost
AI Registration Agent handles MLC submissions, ISRC matching, and co-writer coordination at scale — replaces manual admin that legacy publishers cannot profitably serve
End-to-end recovery registers handa as publisher, generates CWR files, and collects on the writer's behalf — 20% commission only on successfully recovered royalties
Faulty metadata prevents 10%+ of mechanical royalties from reaching creators. $700M currently sits unclaimed at the MLC, with $100M added annually.
By law, unclaimed funds are redistributed to major labels based on market share. In 2027, the MLC begins permanently transferring indie wealth to the Majors.
Publishers charge 15–20% but rarely audit broken registrations. Even "tech" publishers like Kobalt routinely miss $30K+ per writer due to reactive, manual workflows.
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: US first: MLC is the single largest mechanical society with public bulk data and claim tools — no other country has this level of programmatic access
MLC begins redistributing unclaimed blanket royalties from early 2027 — artists who have not claimed by then lose share, creating structural urgency
Every country has its own collecting society with similar registration errors and black boxes — playbook transfers via publisher registration per territory
Unclaimed digital liquidity (The Recoverable Black Box) - starts redistribution in 2027
According to Goldman Sachs (2026) and ASCAP financial reports, the industry is sustaining a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of ~7.3%
Growth is fueled by a 12% annual increase in global streaming subscribers, which is expected to reach 1.2 billion users by 2030
The fastest-growing royalty segment — Copyright Royalty Board (CRB) rate rising to 15.35% of DSP revenue by 2027, the highest in history
Every incumbent focuses on performance royalties. Mechanicals are where the money compounds — and where nobody is looking
The Music Modernization Act triggers redistribution of $700M+ to Universal, Sony, and Warner by market share — unless independent artists claim it first
In addition to the historical $700M, systemic metadata failures add $100M+ in new unclaimed royalties annually, creating a recurring and growing market
slide_4_Market_2_3_Competition: Acquired by Virgin Music Group at $775M / 2011
Legacy publishing admin with 445K writers under UMG/Virgin — strongest incumbent with global coverage (60+ societies) and rights retention
handa’s closest competitor in terms of “Audit-first” UX – easy to see if there are issues and on a subscription model ($96+/year)
AI audit and claims for publishers focused on B2B recovery — proves audit-led category exists in Europe with funded players
AI-driven global rights admin with KOMCA and JASRAC deals — B2B infrastructure layer that validates AI matching approach
Audit-first UX shows specific dollar amounts before asking for anything — competitors charge upfront or show dashboards without actionable recovery and lower conversion
Zero upfront commission-only model undercuts Songtrust $100 signup + commission and Mogul $5-100/month — success-based pricing aligns incentives and removes adoption barrier entirely
Moat must come from matching accuracy, workflow speed, and artist trust not data access — MLC data is public and AI rhetoric is already shared by Muserk and Claimy
slide_5_Market_3_3_GTM_Pricing_BM_Scale: 20% commission on recovered and ongoing royalties; zero upfront cost
First-year ACV ~$1,497 per customer; 2-year contract with rollover
2 signed contracts totaling $39K recovery; 129 leads with $650K pipeline
Dual revenue: retroactive recovery commission plus ongoing publishing admin — per-customer revenue compounds across wave
Community-driven 34% conversion at zero CAC — each signed artist generates word-of-mouth referrals as distribution node
Stage 1: Deploy through 200+ indie producer communities via audit tool — 766 audits and 129 leads with $2.63M identified in 14 days at zero paid marketing cost
Stage 2: Register as publisher with major societies starting MLC then PRS/MCPS and GEMA — each registration unlocks new territory of mechanical recovery revenue
Stage 3: Self-serve publishing platform becomes default admin for indie artists globally — product-led growth with direct-to-DSP deals replaces Collective Management Organization(CMO) layer

Sub-Linear Operational Growth: AI-native registration pipeline eliminates the "human-admin bottleneck"—while legacy publishers require proportional headcount to manage new catalogs, Handa scales to 10x volume with zero additional administrative cost.

Low-CAC Community Flywheel: Community-driven distribution creates a near-zero CAC loop; the audit tool is inherently viral, turning every "found-money" success story into a social proof node that attracts the next cohort of artists.

Territory Expansion: Sequential rollout across global societies reuses the core metadata engine; registering as a publisher territory-by-territory multiplies global revenue streams without expanding the core engineering team.
slide_6_Team_1_4_Composition_&_Roles: CEO Matt is a working music producer with 100M+ streams globally — direct publishing ops experience with MLC, PRS, and MCPS registration means he knows the metadata pain firsthand
Built the handa MVP end-to-end including Python/FastAPI backend, MLC data pipeline, Spotify API integrations, and AI matching engine — technical founder who ships without outside dev
Direct access to 200+ indie producer communities globally provides zero-CAC distribution channel — one group chat drove 449 visitors in 5 days and 766 audits in first 14 days live
Narrowed from broad music platform to mechanical royalty recovery after Bootcamp 1 customer feedback — demonstrated willingness to kill original idea and laser-focus on validated pain
Independent producer; 100M+ streams; 2x Gold and 23x Silver RIAA credits
Built handa MVP end-to-end: Python/FastAPI, MLC pipeline, AI matching
Direct access to 200+ producer communities; zero-CAC distribution proven
slide_7_Team_2_4_Program_Trajectory: Matt joined KOR8 with initial music platform thesis and partnered with Daniel Kim early in the program — Bootcamp 1 Red signal flagged broad scope and unclear problem
Bootcamp 2 with Daniel as co-founder; Jaehee flagged competition and urged immediate customer validation — challenged on why incumbents had not solved this problem yet
Bootcamp 3-1 Green signal from Jaehee; praised MLC focus and outreach momentum — told to proceed to trackout once why-not questions are answered and full cycle tested
Bootcamp 4 scored 9 from Jiho; strong customer description and founder-product fit — conditional approval with scalability roadmap still needed for IC readiness
Group Office Hour scored 9 from Jaehee; execution and cohesion rated 9 — JaeHee Chang confirmed as IC sponsor partner for Investment Committee final assessment
Daniel departed the team during trackout phase; Matt continued as solo CEO — actively recruiting co-founder for customer-facing scale and growth operations
MVP live since April 10; 766 audits and 129 leads in 14 days at zero CAC — 2 signed recovery contracts totaling $39K with $650K pipeline in active conversion
Summarizes the journey from program start through team formation and PMF validation, based on weekly founder retrospectives and program team observations

MVP live, 2 signed contracts $39K, 766 audits, 129 leads in 14 days. Score 9 from Jiho and Jaehee. Zero paid marketing
BC1 Red to BC3-1 Green to BC4 score 9. Co-founder departure handled; continued solo. Killed original idea and rebuilt
100M+ streams as producer with MLC/PRS domain ops firsthand. ICP validated at 34% conversion. Dae Bogan (MLC) endorses
Built full MVP solo: Python/FastAPI, MLC pipeline, AI matching, Spotify API. Domain expertise in metadata and rights
Audit-first UX novel in publishing admin. Commission-only undercuts incumbents. 2027 MLC redistribution timing is strong
Based on weekly founder retrospectives and feedback
slide_9_Team_4_4_Strengths_&_Improvement: Strengths
● Why Now thesis backed by public data
MLC bulk data, 2027 redistribution deadline, and indie growth converge — structural urgency is real and externally verified
● Audit-first GTM is a proven sharp wedge
Showing dollar amounts before asking for anything drives 34% conversion across 766 audits — no competitor matches this motion
● Traction validated with signed contracts
2 signed recovery contracts totaling $39K with 129 leads and $650K pipeline in 14 days — audit-to-contract funnel working
● Commission-only aligns incentives
Zero upfront cost removes adoption friction blocking Songtrust and Mogul — success-based pricing selects recoverable cases
Areas for Improvement
● Solo founder after co-founder departure
Daniel Kim left during trackout — solo CEO must recruit co-founder for customer-facing scale before pipeline outpaces capacity
● Revenue forecast has arithmetic gaps
500 leads x 60% x $1,497 ACV = $449K not $840K target — DD bottom-up conservative estimate is $350-450K Y1 revenue
● Remaining MLC pool quality declining
MLC reports 92% match rate with 95%+ of unmatched under $1 cumulative — easy recoveries shrink as pool quality declines
● Publisher license not yet secured
MLC publisher registration required to execute claims — in progress with Dae Bogan(Head of Third-Party Partnerhips at the MLC) but revenue cannot flow until approved
Team (4/4)
Summary
● No co-founder yet, so equity structure is not determined
Founder Alignment & Equity Structure
slide_10_Investor_Assessment: $10.4B SAM with 2027 statutory rates hitting historic highs (15.35%); market tailwinds are legally mandated
$700M "Black Box" provides a concentrated, high-urgency entry point + $100M added annually
handa's commission-only, audit-first approach undercuts Mogul/Songtrust on price and adoption friction
The lack of a unique technological moat means a fast-follower with similar "Musician-Founder" empathy could attempt to clone the GTM strategy
handa’s AI cleans up messy data much cheaper than legacy players, making it profitable to recover royalties that others ignore
Showing “found money” upfront builds trust in a way incumbents can’t match without admitting they missed it before
34% lookup-to-lead conversion and $2.63M surfaced in 14 days validates a zero-CAC distribution flywheel
39K in signed contracts is currently "unrealized" due to standard industry payout cycles and pending MLC registration
CWR-based "Push" model allows US-built metadata to unlock global territories without linear team growth
Success is contingent on territory-by-territory society registration; can be an administrative bottleneck
Absence of a co-founder for customer-facing scale is the primary operational risk during high-growth phases

handa has built a transparency-led "money-finding" engine that weaponizes the 2027 MLC deadline for rapid market penetration, creating a high-trust user base for a scalable, global digital publishing platform. The long-term value lies in transitioning from a recovery service into the default self-serve publishing platform for independent artists globally.
